reviews.review_msg: Shes a great!!!:)Has a keen sense of knowledge and can be very helpful!

If you are having trouble falling asleep...I strongly recommend taking a class with Maryam and attending her useless lectures...as a matter of fact they shouldn't be called lectures...but rather "reading off the powerpoint slides"...whenever I attended lectures i could stay awake longer than 15 minutes...after that I was zzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz
Teacher was OK. Quiet during lectures, reads off the slides. Midterm was average but final was very hard. Textbook was terrible, class was terrible in general useless labs.

Textbook is like a collection of all the labels from the supermarket shampoo isle. You read through every label of every possible brand and item, then they test you " is it true brand A contains higher alcohol than brand B?" Overall extremely disappointed and required way too much time to learn new things INEFFECTIVELY. Ask around, you'll know.

Publisher quiz Qs include something like "how many hours of project management course a project manager need?" Just a very bad learning experience from this textbook as well as the mymislab online module. Its buggy,only work with IE only,unexpected error and crash ALL THE TIME.Might just use video professor's mail order crap (those are better!!!!)

Prof n material were almost ok. The textbook (pearson exp MIS) was EXTREMELY BAD.The authors did not seem to realize the book is used by many universities MIS course.Content was fuzzy and just a giant collection of jargon n abbrev at best, and all written in grade7 type of style.Publisher quiz tests you  like this is a junior high history class.
The final is really hard! The average grade of the final is only 60%. Professor mainly read the slides during the lectures.

Boring class. Correction: Very boring class. I was hoping it would be fun but it wasn't. Way too much crammed into a semester. Exams are dodgy. They can be deceptively easy. She isn't too knowledgeable herself, once mistaking British universities' TLD for .edu.uk while it should've been .ac.uk . She should've known that, since she studied in the UK

She is very quiet. The material is very dry and she attempts to make it interesting. Midterm and final written were fairly straightforward if you've read your text and notes. The MC was very situational and not definition based. Doing online quizzes help a lot. Lectures are really optional, though she did give out bonus participation marks.
Classes are ok. Exams are hard. MCQ and short answers. Dr. Ali covers material in a lot of detail. Reading business news and technology makes it relevant.
Cool course, nice prof. There is way too much to learn in one semester. She brings in lots of examples, and class exercises that are pretty useful. Memorize the textbook to ace this one.
Lectures are dull, boring, and useless. Her lectures notes are all provided by the text book publisher. All you really need to know is important points from the book.
Prof. Ali is very helpful and approachable. She tries to make the course interesting, although the content can be dry. Study notes and textbook and you will be ok.

MAN She was not good. I stopped attending her lecs halfway. Her ppts are not her notes and she just reads through the points from her ppts (notes are online too, reading her notes and attending lectures are the same thing). Course was based on memorization. Read the textbook, notes are ALL from textbook as well.
